description: An example dashboard for Longhorn. Updated by ONZACK AG (Robin Hermann)

panels.description: The total number of volumes in the Longhorn storage system
panels.title: Total Number Of Volumes

panels.description: Healthy volumes are volumes that are attaching to a node and have the number of healthy replicas equals to the expected number of replicas.
panels.title: Number Of Healthy Volumes

panels.description: Note that Longhorn volume actual size is not the size of the filesystem inside a Longhorn volume.  See more at : https://longhorn.io/docs/1.0.2/volumes-and-nodes/volume-size/#volume-actual-size
panels.title: Volume Actual Size

panels.description: Degraded volumes are volumes that have the number of healthy replicas smaller than the expected number of replicas. e.g. User creates a volume with 2 replicas but 1 replicas is failed.
panels.title: Number Of Degraded Volumes

panels.description: Fault volumes are volumes that doesn't have any healthy replica.
panels.title: Number Of Fault Volumes

panels.description: The capacity of each Longhorn volume

targets.expr:
100 *
  label_replace(
    avg by (volume, namespace, persistentvolumeclaim) (kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes), "pvc", "$1", "persistentvolumeclaim", "(.*)"
  )
/ on (namespace, pvc)
  label_replace(
    avg by (pvc_namespace, pvc) (
      longhorn_volume_capacity_bytes{pvc_namespace=~"$namespace",pvc=~"$pvc",volume=~"$pv_name"} and on (volume) (longhorn_volume_state != 3)
    ),
    "namespace", "$1", "pvc_namespace", "(.*)"
  )

panels.title: Volume Capacity

panels.description: Attached volumes are volumes that are currently attaching to a node
panels.title: Number Of Attached Volumes

panels.description: Detached volumes are volumes that aren't currently attaching to a node
panels.title: Number Of Detached Volumes

panels.description: Longhorn volumes bound to a PVC in the selected Namespace/PVC scope. These are the volumes we expect to be backed up. NOTE: Longhorn does not export backup POLICY as a metric - this dashboard measures observed backup COVERAGE (via longhorn_volume_last_backup_at).
panels.title: Volumes (backup-expected)

panels.description: Volumes with a successful backup within the 'Stale after' window.
panels.title: With Recent Backup

panels.description: Volumes that DO have backups, but the newest one is older than the 'Stale after' window - backups are falling behind schedule.
panels.title: Stale Backup

panels.description: Backup-expected volumes that have NEVER been backed up (longhorn_volume_last_backup_at == 0). Either not covered by a backup policy, or the first run hasn't happened yet. Deselect known-OK namespaces/PVCs above to keep this at 0.
panels.title: Without Any Backup

panels.description: Longhorn Backup CRs currently in the Error state (longhorn_backup_state == 4), for the selected Backup Job(s) and Namespace/PVC scope.
panels.title: Failed Backup Jobs

panels.description: Age of the oldest successful backup among backup-expected volumes.
panels.title: Oldest Backup Age

panels.description: Every backup-expected Longhorn PVC and the age of its last successful backup. 'Never' (red) = no backup has ever completed for this volume. Sorted worst-first.
panels.title: PVC Backup Coverage

panels.description: Per recurring backup job (backup 'group'): number of Backup CRs tracked, how many are in Error state, and total storage consumed. Filtered by the Backup Job variable. Counts ALL Backup CRs including orphaned ones (volumes already deleted) - so 'Failed' here can exceed the 'Failed Backup Jobs' stat, which only counts failures for volumes that still exist. '(manual / no recurring job)' = backups not created by a recurring job.

targets.expr:
label_replace(
  (count by (recurring_job) (longhorn_backup_state{recurring_job=~"$recurring_job"} == 4))
  or
  (0 * count by (recurring_job) (longhorn_backup_state{recurring_job=~"$recurring_job"}))
, "recurring_job", "(manual / no recurring job)", "recurring_job", "^$")

panels.title: Backup Jobs Overview

panels.description: Longhorn Backup CRs currently in Error (4) or Unknown (5) state, mapped to their PVC. Filtered by Backup Job + Namespace/PVC. Backup CR names (generic UIDs) are omitted; recurring_job shows which schedule produced it ('' = manual/on-demand).
panels.title: Backup Jobs Needing Attention

panels.description: Total backup storage consumed per PVC (sum of all backup revisions) on the external backup target. Filtered by Backup Job + Namespace/PVC.
panels.title: Backup Storage Used by PVC

panels.description: The total number of nodes in the Longhorn storage system
panels.title: Total Number Of Nodes

panels.description: Disabled nodes are nodes that are disabled by the user.  When users disable a node, Longhorn will not use the node's storage for replica scheduling. Note that Longhorn can still attach a volume to disabled nodes because the actual data of the volume could be on a different node.
panels.title: Number Of Disabled Nodes

panels.description: Schedulable nodes are nodes that Longhorn can use their storage for replica scheduling.

targets.expr: (count(longhorn_node_status{condition="schedulable"}==1) OR on() vector(0)) - (count(longhorn_node_status{condition="allowScheduling"}==0) OR on() vector(0))

panels.title: Number Of Schedulable Nodes

panels.description: Failed Nodes are nodes that Longhorn cannot attach volumes to and cannot schedule replicas onto. e.g: when the nodes went down.

targets.expr: (avg(longhorn_node_count_total) OR on() vector(0)) - (count(longhorn_node_status{condition="ready"}==1) OR on() vector(0))

panels.title: Number Of Failed Nodes

panels.description: The capacity of each Longhorn volume
panels.title: Disk Space Usage

panels.description: Instance managers are pods that contains the engine and replica processes of Longhorn volumes. See more at https://longhorn.io/docs/1.0.2/concepts/#11-the-longhorn-manager-and-the-longhorn-engine
panels.title: Instance Manager CPU Usage

panels.description: Instance managers are pods that contains the engine and replica processes of Longhorn volumes. See more at https://longhorn.io/docs/1.0.2/concepts/#11-the-longhorn-manager-and-the-longhorn-engine
panels.title: Instance Manager CPU Used from Request

panels.description: Instance managers are pods that contains the engine and replica processes of Longhorn volumes. See more at https://longhorn.io/docs/1.0.2/concepts/#11-the-longhorn-manager-and-the-longhorn-engine
panels.title: Instance Manager Memory Usage

panels.description: Longhorn manager pods manage the control plane of the Longhorn system. e.g. Volume scheduling, attaching, detaching, backup, etc,..
panels.title: Longhorn Manager CPU Usage

panels.description: Longhorn manager pods manage the control plane of the Longhorn system. e.g. Volume scheduling, attaching, detaching, backup, etc,..
panels.title: Longhorn Manager Memory Usage

panels.description: To get Longhorn Alerts you have to deploy PrometheusRules (more Infos: https://longhorn.io/docs/1.3.1/monitoring/alert-rules-example/)
panels.title: Longhorn Alerts

list.description: Namespace filter. Scopes the whole dashboard (volume + backup panels). Deselect a namespace to exclude it.
list.label: Namespace
list.name: namespace

list.description: PVC filter (friendly names, chained to the selected namespace).
list.label: PVC
list.name: pvc

list.description: PV filter (UUID, chained to the selected namespace and pvc-name).
list.label: PV
list.name: pv_name

list.description: Backup job/group filter (the recurring job that created each backup; empty = manual/on-demand). Drives the backup-job panels. NOTE: the per-volume coverage metric has no recurring_job label, so the coverage panels are filtered by Namespace/PVC only.
list.label: Backup Job
list.name: recurring_job

list.description: A backed-up PVC is considered stale if its last successful backup is older than this.
list.label: Stale after
list.name: stale_after

title: Longhorn Monitoring & Backups
